This advisory has been withdrawn because it is a duplicate of GHSA-ccwh-wwpp-6wg5. This link is maintained to preserve external references.

## Original Description

OpenClaw before 2026.5.26 contains an insufficient sanitization vulnerability in the host environment sanitizer that allows Node.js control variables to bypass validation. Attackers with access to workspace .env files, tool environment overrides, or skill environment blocks can pass malicious Node.js control variables to influence child processes or coverage output paths.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-vr6h-vxqj-3pjx", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-18T13:02:39Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vr6h-vxqj-3pjx", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-vr6h-vxqj-3pjx"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-16T19:17:04Z", "published_at": "2026-06-16T21:32:00Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-ccwh-wwpp-6wg5", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-53864", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-insufficient-environment-variable-sanitization-in-node-js-control-variables", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vr6h-vxqj-3pjx"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "Duplicate Advisory: Host environment sanitizer missed two Node.js control variables", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-18T13:02:40Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-vr6h-vxqj-3pjx", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2026.5.22"}], "withdrawn_at": "2026-06-18T13:02:39Z"}
